CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-03-2024 Set For Trial On Apr-01-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HENRY WYKOWSKI, AN INDIVIDUAL AND WYKOWSKI & WOOD, LLP's DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT

Overruled.

The demurrer attacks the first cause of action for breach of contract. It argues that the claim fails due to non-compliance with California Rule of Professional Conduct 1.5.1 and B+P 6147.

As plaintiff notes, CRPC 1.5.1 applies only if the lawyers "are not in the same law firm." B+P 6147 contemplates agreements between clients and lawyers. Plaintiff claims he himself did not contract with the client: the agreement at issue is between him and the defendant firm, and he was at material times working as part of the firm, and was not in a different firm. The FAC is replete with allegations that support that view, which are duplicated in the Opposition. Obviously, the truth of the allegations is assumed here, and defendant's moving papers make no argument that as a matter of law all those allegations together cannot support the conclusion that plaintiff worked in the defendant's firm.

Although not clearly delineated as a separate argument, the demurrer also seems to allege that the operative first amended complaint fails to comply with CCP 430.10 (g), see Memorandum at 4:25-27. This argument notes the FAC's allegations of a mixture of express and oral provisions (FAC 11), and suggests that the FAC does not set out the terms of the agreement. The terms, however, are set out at FAC 10-11. The defense's invocation of 430.10 (g) can be seen as an objection that the FAC doesn't tell us which parts of the agreement are oral, or written, or implied in fact; but, being generous as we must be on demurrer, the FAC indeed does do that. However, if there are terms of the agreement not stated in the FAC, the plaintiff may wish to seek leave (or an agreement from defendant) to amend to add them, but for now the court and defendant should assume all the material terms are set out in the FAC.

Defendants must answer the complaint not later than August 3, 2024.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/CEK)
